pageMeta.title: AI Agents for Home Medical Equipment Support
pageMeta.description: DocsBot helps home medical equipment teams answer product, setup, coverage, and service questions faster with source-grounded AI agents.

hero.eyebrow: AI automation for DME, supplies, service, and patient support
hero.headline: Equip home medical customers with safer, faster AI support
hero.subheadline: DocsBot helps home medical equipment providers create AI agents trained on approved manuals, FAQs, service policies, reimbursement guidance, onboarding documents, and internal SOPs—so customers, caregivers, sales teams, and support staff can get source-grounded answers and escalate sensitive cases to a person.

proofBullets.text: Answer setup, cleaning, warranty, rental, replacement, and delivery questions from approved product manuals and policies.
Collect intake details, qualify product-fit questions, route service requests, and prepare human handoffs with conversation context.
Give staff a private knowledge agent for SOPs, payer workflows, documentation checklists, and field-service troubleshooting.

hero.primaryCta: Build a home medical equipment agent

businessClarity.whatDocsBotDoes: DocsBot turns your approved HME knowledge into AI support agents and internal knowledge agents. Train it on product manuals, supplier policies, website content, PDFs, SOPs, reimbursement FAQs, delivery workflows, and helpdesk history when connected, then use it to answer questions, draft responses, summarize cases, identify knowledge gaps, and trigger bounded workflows through customer-configured Skills, APIs, connectors, or AI Actions.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Help patients, caregivers, referral sources, and family members get clear answers without forcing staff to repeat the same equipment, eligibility, delivery, and maintenance explanations all day.

bullets.text: Respond from approved sources when customers ask about CPAP supplies, mobility equipment, oxygen accessories, hospital beds, or recurring orders.
Collect missing order, prescription, insurance, address, and service-request details before a human reviews the case.
Escalate clinical, safety-critical, billing-dispute, or coverage-denial questions to trained staff with a concise summary.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Standardize answers across branches while keeping regulated workflows bounded by approved documentation, permissions, review paths, and logs.

bullets.text: CMS says Medicare DMEPOS suppliers must obtain accreditation, enroll as DMEPOS suppliers, and post a surety bond to receive Medicare reimbursement.
Make supplier standards, setup procedures, maintenance policies, and documentation checklists easier for staff to retrieve.
Use analytics and conversation review to find repeated questions, outdated answers, and training gaps across teams.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Support presales conversations with referral partners, case managers, discharge planners, and caregivers while keeping humans responsible for final recommendations, pricing, payer commitments, and regulated decisions.

bullets.text: Answer product, service-area, accessory, rental, delivery, and documentation questions from current catalogs and approved sales materials.
Qualify leads with structured intake fields such as equipment need, location, timeline, payer type, referral source, and preferred follow-up.
Draft handoff summaries for CRM, email, helpdesk, or team workflows when configured through Skills, APIs, connectors, or forms.

evidenceSources.metric: DMEPOS suppliers must meet Medicare enrollment requirements.
evidenceSources.claim: CMS states that suppliers receiving Medicare reimbursement for DMEPOS must obtain DMEPOS accreditation, enroll in Medicare as a DMEPOS supplier, and post a surety bond.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: Enroll as a DMEPOS Supplier | CMS

evidenceSources.metric: Medicare Part B covers multiple DMEPOS benefit categories.
evidenceSources.claim: CMS describes DMEPOS categories including DME such as hospital beds, wheelchairs, ventilators, and oxygen equipment, as well as prosthetics, orthotics, surgical dressings, therapeutic shoes, and lymphedema compression items.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: Durable Medical Equipment, Prosthetic Devices, Prosthetics, Orthotics, & Supplies | CMS

evidenceSources.metric: DMEPOS suppliers must meet CMS quality standards to keep billing privileges unless exempt.
evidenceSources.claim: CMS MLN guidance says DMEPOS suppliers must meet CMS DMEPOS Quality Standards and become accredited to get or keep Medicare billing privileges unless they are exempt.

evidenceSources.metric: Home-use medical devices create unique safety and usability questions.
evidenceSources.claim: The FDA notes that home-use device considerations include instructions for use, device breakage, recalls, cleaning, hazardous waste disposal, prescription questions, and home wiring compatibility.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: Frequently Asked Questions About Home Use Devices | FDA

evidenceSources.metric: Home settings add environmental and safety considerations for medical devices.
evidenceSources.claim: FDA guidance highlights environmental considerations and potential safety hazards that providers and patients should consider when using or evaluating a medical device in the home.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: Unique Considerations in the Home | FDA

evidenceSources.metric: 52 million Americans were age 65 or older in 2018.
evidenceSources.claim: The U.S. Census Bureau reported 52 million people age 65 and older in 2018 and projected that older adults would outnumber children under 18 for the first time in U.S. history by 2034.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: By 2030, All Baby Boomers Will Be Age 65 or Older | U.S. Census Bureau

agentUseCases.name: Product and setup answer agent
agentUseCases.problem: HME teams support a broad mix of hospital beds, wheelchairs, oxygen equipment, ventilators, orthotics, dressings, shoes, and compression items, which makes consistent product guidance difficult across channels.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: Train a DocsBot agent on approved product manuals, setup guides, cleaning instructions, warranty policies, rental rules, reorder FAQs, and service-area pages. The agent answers common questions with source-grounded guidance, cites the underlying content, and escalates unclear or safety-sensitive issues to staff.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: With configured Skills or APIs, the agent can look up order status, delivery windows, supply eligibility, or service-ticket status, then route unresolved issues to the right queue.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: How do I clean this CPAP mask cushion?
Can this wheelchair fit through a 28-inch doorway?
What should I do if my hospital bed remote stops working?
When is my next resupply eligible?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Reduce repetitive calls while giving customers and caregivers clearer first-line answers from the materials your team already approves.

agentUseCases.name: Coverage, documentation, and intake assistant
agentUseCases.problem: Customers and referral partners often need to know which documents, prescriptions, order details, and payer information are required before staff can move an HME request forward.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot can guide users through a structured intake flow, answer documentation FAQs from approved reimbursement and supplier policies, collect missing details, and prepare a summarized handoff for billing, intake, or customer service review.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: AI Actions can create a task or ticket, update approved fields, notify a team, or forward an intake summary to configured CRM, helpdesk, email, or form workflows.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: What information do you need for a power mobility order?
Can you send my intake details to your billing team?
What documents are usually needed before delivery?
I need supplies for a family member; what should I gather first?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Improve intake completeness and reduce back-and-forth while keeping coverage decisions, medical necessity, and payer commitments under human review.

agentUseCases.name: Home-use safety and escalation guide
agentUseCases.problem: Patients use medical devices in varied home environments, where cleaning, recalls, disposal, wiring, breakage, and setup conditions can affect safe use.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: Train the agent on approved safety checklists, device instructions, recall-response policies, cleaning guides, escalation rules, and manufacturer documentation. The agent gives general support from approved sources, asks clarifying questions, and routes safety-critical issues to trained staff.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: A configured Skill can create an urgent service ticket, capture device model and serial number, attach conversation context, and notify the appropriate team without letting the agent make clinical judgments.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: My oxygen concentrator alarm is sounding—what information should I give your technician?
Where do I find recall information for this device?
How do I dispose of used tubing or sharps safely?
Is there anything I should check before using this device at home?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Give customers a clearer path for urgent and non-urgent device questions while preserving human oversight for safety-critical issues.

agentUseCases.name: Internal SOP and field-service knowledge agent
agentUseCases.problem: Dispatch, warehouse, billing, intake, and field-service teams rely on many SOPs, branch policies, equipment setup steps, and payer-specific checklists that are hard to search quickly.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot can act as a private internal knowledge assistant trained on SOPs, training materials, PDFs, wikis, policies, manufacturer references, and service playbooks. Staff ask natural-language questions and receive source-grounded answers with links back to the approved source.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: When authorized, Skills, APIs, connectors, or an MCP Connector can retrieve live ticket metadata, branch details, or equipment records so the agent can summarize context before a technician or supervisor acts.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: What is our setup checklist for a semi-electric hospital bed?
Which form do I use for a respiratory equipment pickup?
What are the steps for documenting a failed delivery?
Summarize this service ticket for the technician.
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Help employees find the right procedure faster, reduce tribal knowledge dependence, and surface documentation gaps through analytics.

agentUseCases.name: Referral and presales qualification agent
agentUseCases.problem: Referral partners, discharge planners, and caregivers ask service-fit questions before they are ready for a full order, and sales teams need structured context before follow-up.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: A DocsBot presales agent can answer approved questions about service areas, equipment categories, rental options, accessories, delivery expectations, and required next steps. It can collect lead details and apply customer-defined qualification rules for urgency, location, equipment category, payer type, and follow-up channel.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: With approved workflows, the agent can submit a custom lead form, create a CRM task, draft a follow-up email, or request a meeting through configured scheduling tools such as Calendly, Cal.com, TidyCal, or similar systems.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Do you deliver hospital beds to my ZIP code?
Can someone explain rental versus purchase options?
We are discharging a patient tomorrow—can you route this to the right team?
Book a consultation with your equipment specialist.
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Capture demand from website visitors and referral sources while giving sales or intake staff better-prepared conversations.

agentUseCases.name: Knowledge-gap and support trend analysis
agentUseCases.problem: High-volume support teams may not know which products, payer steps, manuals, or service issues are driving the most confusion until calls and tickets pile up.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot analytics, logs, topic management, reports, conversation review, satisfaction and resolution analysis, and knowledge-gap discovery help teams see repeated questions and improve source content over time.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: When helpdesk exports or configured ticket workflows are available, the agent can summarize recurring themes and draft internal documentation updates for human review.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Which oxygen supply questions came up most this month?
What topics did the agent escalate most often?
Which answer sources need updates?
Summarize unresolved website conversations for the support manager.
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Turn support conversations into operational feedback for better FAQs, training, manuals, and intake workflows.

customerFacingAgent.headline: Give patients, caregivers, and referral partners an always-available equipment guide
customerFacingAgent.intro: A DocsBot customer-facing agent can live in your website or support experience and answer from approved HME content. It can provide first-line guidance, collect details, route requests, and escalate when a question involves clinical judgment, payer decisions, safety concerns, or complex service issues.

requests.request: “I need replacement supplies. What information do you need from me?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Explain the approved intake process, ask for configured fields, summarize the request, and route it to the right support or billing workflow when authorized.
requests.optionalAction: Create a helpdesk ticket or task with the customer’s details and a conversation summary.

requests.request: “My device is not working and I’m not sure if it is urgent.”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Provide non-diagnostic troubleshooting from approved manuals, ask for model and symptom details, present escalation instructions, and immediately hand off safety-sensitive situations.
requests.optionalAction: Open an urgent service request and notify the configured team with device details and transcript context.

requests.request: “Do you carry equipment for home recovery after surgery?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Answer from the product catalog and service policies, clarify location and equipment need, and avoid medical recommendations beyond approved content.
requests.optionalAction: Collect lead details and forward a qualified summary to sales, intake, or referral coordination.

requests.request: “How do I clean or maintain this item?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Retrieve the relevant product guide, summarize the approved cleaning or maintenance steps, and cite the source so the customer can verify details.
requests.optionalAction: Offer a handoff to support if the customer reports damage, contamination, malfunction, or missing instructions.

requests.request: “Can I get help in another language?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Use DocsBot multilingual answering capabilities to respond from the same approved knowledge base while preserving escalation paths for complex cases.
requests.optionalAction: Route the conversation to a multilingual staff queue or human review workflow when configured.

requests.agentResponseStrategy: If connected to approved systems, use live context to retrieve delivery or order status; if not, collect the details needed for staff follow-up.
requests.optionalAction: Look up status through a configured Skill or create a callback task for dispatch.

internalAgent.headline: Keep HME staff aligned on procedures, policies, and service workflows
internalAgent.intro: DocsBot can also operate as a private internal knowledge agent for intake, billing, dispatch, warehouse, field service, compliance, and sales teams. Staff can ask questions in private chat, Slack, Microsoft Teams, or other configured experiences and get answers from approved internal sources.

workflows.name: Billing and documentation lookup
workflows.problem: Teams need fast access to payer rules, supplier standards, order requirements, and branch-specific documentation steps without relying on memory.
workflows.agentWorkflow: Train DocsBot on approved billing SOPs, supplier standards, payer notes, forms, and internal policies. Staff ask questions and receive source-grounded answers with links back to the relevant document.
workflows.optionalAction: Draft a checklist or case summary for human review before submission.
workflows.outcome: Reduce avoidable delays caused by missing or inconsistent documentation.

workflows.name: Dispatch and field-service preparation
workflows.problem: Technicians and dispatch teams need equipment-specific setup steps, pickup rules, delivery notes, and troubleshooting paths before they enter the home.
workflows.agentWorkflow: The internal agent retrieves setup procedures, service scripts, manufacturer references, and pickup policies, then summarizes what the technician needs to know for the job.
workflows.optionalAction: Summarize the service record and generate a technician briefing when live context is connected.
workflows.outcome: Help teams deliver more consistent service while keeping final field decisions with trained staff.

workflows.name: Sales and referral enablement
workflows.problem: Account managers and intake coordinators need fast answers to product, service-area, objection-handling, and referral-process questions.
workflows.agentWorkflow: Train DocsBot on sales playbooks, service catalogs, referral FAQs, pricing guidance for internal use, case studies, and approved follow-up language. Reps can ask for product fit notes or drafted email responses.
workflows.optionalAction: Draft a follow-up email, create a CRM task, or summarize a referral conversation through customer-configured workflows.
workflows.outcome: Give sales teams more consistent, policy-aligned messaging without automating regulated commitments.

workflows.name: Compliance-aware content review
workflows.problem: Outdated manuals, forms, and internal instructions can create inconsistent answers across branches and channels.
workflows.agentWorkflow: Use DocsBot logs, reports, topic management, and conversation review to identify repeated unanswered questions, outdated sources, and content gaps. Teams can then update the approved knowledge base.
workflows.optionalAction: Generate a draft FAQ or SOP update for human review before publishing.
workflows.outcome: Improve support content continuously while maintaining source control and review boundaries.

trustClaims: Healthcare teams can use DocsBot to streamline internal decision-making with source-grounded knowledge access.
DocsBot can train on owned manuals, websites, and content libraries for scalable 24/7 support.
Governed AI agents support sensitive workflows with permissions, logs, encryption, and human escalation paths.
trustAndProof.whyTheseReviewsFit: Home medical equipment teams need accurate answers from approved materials, dependable internal knowledge, and careful escalation for sensitive requests. DocsBot proof spans healthcare communications, manual-based product support, and owned-content training—matching the operational demands of HME support and service teams.

backgroundImage.alt: white and black earbuds on white textile

faq.question: Can the agent answer product questions without giving medical advice or making equipment recommendations it should not make?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can be trained on approved manuals, product catalogs, setup guides, FAQs, and service policies, then instructed to answer only from those sources. For product-fit, clinical, safety, payer, or medical-necessity questions, the agent can collect context and escalate to trained staff instead of making a final recommendation.

faq.question: How would this help our intake team with incomplete orders, missing prescriptions, or unclear payer details?
faq.answer: DocsBot can guide patients, caregivers, and referral sources through structured intake questions, collect missing details, and summarize the request for billing or intake review. It can explain your approved documentation process, but humans remain responsible for coverage decisions, medical necessity review, payer commitments, and exception handling.

faq.question: Can DocsBot support DMEPOS compliance workflows without becoming a compliance risk?
faq.answer: DocsBot is best used as a source-grounded retrieval and workflow assistant: it can surface approved supplier standards, documentation checklists, SOPs, and escalation rules while keeping final compliance decisions with your team. That matters because CMS says Medicare DMEPOS suppliers must obtain accreditation, enroll as DMEPOS suppliers, and post a surety bond to receive reimbursement.

faq.question: What kinds of HME questions are safe to automate first?
faq.answer: Good first use cases are repetitive, policy-based questions: cleaning instructions, warranty rules, rental versus purchase basics, resupply intake requirements, delivery expectations, service-area questions, and where to find model or serial numbers. Safety-sensitive device failures, billing disputes, coverage denials, and clinical questions should route to a person with a conversation summary.

faq.question: Can the agent handle our broad catalog across oxygen, CPAP, mobility, beds, supplies, and accessories?
faq.answer: Yes, if your approved source content is organized and kept current. DocsBot can train on manuals, PDFs, FAQs, product pages, and policies across equipment categories. CMS describes DMEPOS categories that include items such as hospital beds, wheelchairs, ventilators, oxygen equipment, prosthetics, orthotics, surgical dressings, therapeutic shoes, and lymphedema compression items.

faq.question: How does DocsBot prevent outdated manuals or branch-specific policies from creating wrong answers?
faq.answer: DocsBot answers from the knowledge sources you approve, so implementation should include source ownership, review cycles, branch tagging where needed, and escalation rules for unclear answers. Analytics, logs, topic management, conversation review, and knowledge-gap discovery help teams see which sources are missing, stale, or driving repeated escalations.

faq.question: Can we connect the agent to order status, delivery scheduling, resupply eligibility, or service tickets?
faq.answer: Yes, when your team configures the right Skills, APIs, connectors, MCP Connector, or workflow automation. DocsBot can perform bounded lookups, create tasks or tickets, update approved fields, notify teams, and prepare handoffs. The key is scoping each action so the agent only accesses authorized systems and performs approved steps.

faq.question: Will this work with our helpdesk or ticketing process if we already use a system for calls, emails, and service requests?
faq.answer: DocsBot can support helpdesk-connected workflows through customer-configured Skills, APIs, connectors, exports, or workflow access. It can summarize conversations, draft responses, route tickets, or create handoff notes, but you should keep human review for safety issues, billing disputes, payer commitments, and unusual service situations.

faq.question: How would we measure ROI beyond saying the agent reduced call volume?
faq.answer: Track repetitive question deflection, intake completeness, after-hours captures, escalation rate, average handle time, first-contact resolution, abandoned inquiries, callback reduction, staff search time, and documentation gaps resolved. For HME, also compare before-and-after trends by equipment category, branch, payer workflow, and service-request type.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help caregivers and older patients who may not know the exact equipment name?
faq.answer: Yes. The agent can ask clarifying questions, guide users to find model numbers or supply details, and route uncertain cases to staff. It can also support multilingual answering from the same approved knowledge base. Demand for clear support matters as the U.S. Census Bureau reported 52 million Americans age 65 or older in 2018 and projected older adults would outnumber children under 18 by 2034.

faq.question: How should we handle device alarms, breakage, recalls, cleaning, or disposal questions?
faq.answer: Train the agent on approved manufacturer instructions, recall-response procedures, safety checklists, cleaning guides, and escalation rules. It can provide non-diagnostic guidance, collect device details, and create an urgent handoff when configured. The FDA notes home-use device questions can involve instructions, breakage, recalls, cleaning, hazardous waste disposal, prescriptions, and home wiring compatibility.

faq.question: Can we use it internally for billing, dispatch, warehouse, and field-service SOPs?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can act as a private internal knowledge agent trained on SOPs, payer notes, forms, setup procedures, pickup rules, branch policies, service playbooks, and manufacturer references. Staff can ask natural-language questions and receive source-grounded answers with links back to the approved document.

faq.question: What controls are available for sensitive customer, patient, or payer information?
faq.answer: DocsBot offers SOC 2 Type II certification, GDPR-ready governance, encryption in transit and at rest, granular access controls, role-based permissions, and does not use customer data to train public models. For live actions, Skills can be scoped with secrets management, metadata bindings, logging, network policy, and customer-defined boundaries.

faq.question: Can the agent answer Medicare or insurance coverage questions?
faq.answer: It can answer from your approved reimbursement FAQs, supplier policies, and documentation checklists, but it should not promise coverage, approve medical necessity, or override payer review. A good setup is to explain general document requirements, collect missing information, and hand the case to billing or intake staff for final review.

faq.question: How much work is required before launch if our knowledge is spread across PDFs, website pages, shared drives, and staff memory?
faq.answer: Most teams start by prioritizing high-volume content: product manuals, cleaning guides, delivery policies, rental FAQs, resupply intake instructions, escalation rules, and top SOPs. DocsBot can train on websites, PDFs, documents, wikis, and internal content, but staff-owned tribal knowledge should be converted into approved articles before it is used for customer-facing answers.

faq.question: Can we give different answers to customers, intake staff, billing, and field technicians?
faq.answer: Yes. You can separate customer-facing and internal agents, restrict sources by role, and use role-based permissions for private content. For example, customers might see delivery and cleaning guidance, while billing staff can access payer documentation checklists and technicians can access setup SOPs, pickup procedures, and service notes.

faq.question: How does DocsBot support referral partners and discharge planners without overcommitting our team?
faq.answer: A presales or referral agent can answer approved service-area, equipment-category, rental, delivery, and documentation questions, then collect structured details such as ZIP code, equipment need, timeline, payer type, referral source, and preferred follow-up. It can route urgent or qualified requests to sales or intake while leaving pricing, payer, and delivery commitments to staff.

faq.question: Can the agent book consultations or create CRM follow-up tasks for qualified referrals?
faq.answer: Yes, with customer-configured Skills, APIs, connectors, forms, or scheduling workflows. DocsBot can collect scheduling intent, submit lead details, draft follow-up emails, create CRM-style tasks, or request a meeting through configured scheduling tools. Humans should still review complex pricing, contracts, regulated services, and high-impact commitments.

faq.question: What if a customer asks something the agent cannot find in our approved sources?
faq.answer: DocsBot should be configured to say when it does not have enough information, ask for clarifying details if useful, and route the question to a human. Those unresolved conversations are also valuable: analytics and knowledge-gap discovery can show which manuals, FAQs, policies, or SOPs need updates.

faq.question: Can we review conversations for quality, compliance, and training opportunities?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot provides logs, analytics, reports, topic management, conversation review, satisfaction and resolution analysis, and knowledge-gap discovery. HME teams can review escalations by product line, branch, payer step, delivery issue, or service category, then update source content and staff training.

faq.question: Will staff trust the agent, or will they see it as another system to check?
faq.answer: Adoption improves when the agent cites sources, links back to approved documents, and answers the questions employees already ask supervisors every day. Start with internal SOP lookup, dispatch preparation, and billing checklists, then use staff feedback and conversation review to improve the content before expanding automation.

faq.question: How do we compare DocsBot with a generic website chatbot or a custom-built AI project?
faq.answer: For HME, the differentiators are source-grounded answers, private internal knowledge use cases, governed actions, escalation paths, analytics, and security controls. A generic bot may capture messages, while DocsBot can answer from approved manuals and SOPs, summarize cases, identify knowledge gaps, and trigger scoped workflows when configured.

faq.question: Can this support multiple branches, service areas, and different local policies?
faq.answer: Yes, if your content and permissions reflect those differences. DocsBot can be structured around approved branch policies, service-area pages, local delivery rules, and internal SOPs. For live actions, customer-configured Skills or APIs can use authorized context such as branch, ZIP code, equipment category, or queue routing rules.

faq.question: What should we avoid automating in the first phase?
faq.answer: Avoid fully automating clinical judgment, emergency triage, payer approvals, billing disputes, denial appeals, recall decisions, and technician-only safety calls. Use the agent to gather details, retrieve approved guidance, create a ticket, and escalate. Home settings add safety and environmental considerations for medical devices, according to FDA guidance.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help us keep Medicare supplier standards and accreditation-related procedures easier to find?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can serve as an internal knowledge agent for supplier standards, accreditation checklists, forms, billing SOPs, and training materials. That is useful because CMS MLN guidance says DMEPOS suppliers must meet CMS DMEPOS Quality Standards and become accredited to get or keep Medicare billing privileges unless exempt.
